title: Gantu

{{Quote|Gantu would steal supplies of honey…and when that was gone his appetite would turn to the taste of Ewok people!|Logray|<ref name="Ewoks 1">[[Ewoks (1985) 1|''Ewoks'' (1985) 1]]</ref>}}
'''Gantu''' was an [[Ogre]] native to the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] around the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Gantu had little use for work, typically acted only for self-interest, and was a short-tempered individual—though he was also afraid of the dark. The Ogre was known to the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] and [[Zandor Rocker]]s as a threat and a menace, as he was prone to raiding [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] and stealing the Ewoks' [[Honey/Legends|honey]], as well as eating some of the Ewoks. He also stole all of the [[Glowing nectar|sunnydew nectar]] from the village of the Zandor Rockers.

One of his few defeats came at the hands of [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]] and his friend [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] when they stole his sunnydew nectar to save their friend, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa]], from an illness. Though Gantu attempted to stop the two Ewoks, they were able to defeat him by capitalizing on his greed for the [[Bridge crystal|Rainbow Crystal]]. They distracted him with the crystal, which would enable the Ogre to cross the [[Gorge of Fire]], throwing it between them, until it eventually broke. Without the Rainbow Crystal, Gantu was unable to cross the Gorge and never attacked Bright Tree Village again.

==Biography==
===Residence===
{{Quote|Long ago we were plagued by the evil Ogre, Gantu, who lived in the barren land of Zandor.|Logray|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
Gantu was an [[Ogre]] who lived on the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]]. Sometime before or during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he discovered a cave in the land of [[Zandor]], and continued to reside in this cave for the rest of his life. Also around this time, he came into possession of a special [[Bridge crystal|rainbow crystal]]. The crystal, if held toward Endor's [[Star/Legends|sun]], [[Ibleam]], would create a bridge that appeared to be made of rainbows. This proved useful to Gantu when he attempted to cross the [[Gorge of Fire]], a barrier that isolated Zandor from the rest of Endor.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

After coming into possession of the crystal, the Ogre was able to raid the villages of the [[Zandor Rocker]]s, a species of sentient rocks that lived on Endor. Gantu was able to steal [[sunnydew flower]]s from the Rockers, which gave him [[Glowing nectar|sunnydew nectar]], a substance valued for its healing powers. However, Gantu used the nectar for a different purpose—to use its glowing power for nocturnal illumination, as the Ogre was afraid of the darkness associated with night.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

===Raiding Bright Tree Village===
{{Quote|One day, a young Chief Chirpa followed the Ogre back to his cave!|Logray|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
[[File:GantuEatEwoks-ME1.png|thumb|left|250px|Gantu raids Bright Tree Village]]
Gantu, using the bridge to cross the Gorge of Fire, found his way to another section of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] called [[Happy Grove/Legends|Happy Grove]]. It was here he discovered [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], which was the home of the native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. For reasons unknown, Gantu hated the Ewoks and raided their village any chance he could get. Stealing [[Honey/Legends|honey]] and other supplies from the Ewoks, he earned the hatred of the village. Later, the Ogre even began to consume the Ewoks themselves. Bright Tree Village's young [[Chief/Legends|Chief]], [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], followed the Ogre back to his cave, following the rainbow bridge, and learned the Ogre's secrets. As the Ogre slept, Chirpa stole the crystal and some of the nectar, though at that time Chirpa was unaware of the nectar's unique properties.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

The following day, Gantu awoke, realizing these possessions were gone. He was unable to cross the Gorge of Fire, as Chirpa possessed the crystal, which allowed him to cross it. Chirpa returned to the Ewok village and gave to his fellow villagers the crystal, which was placed in the [[Royal Hut|Council Hut]] and guarded by the Ewok [[Ebab]]. After a few years, Gantu had become nothing more than a legend to the Ewoks.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

===Wicket and Teebo's escape===
{{Quote|Teebo, we're trapped!|Wicket|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
[[File:Gantu_angry.png|thumb|240px|Gantu chases after Teebo]]
After [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], the daughter of Chief Chirpa, was poisoned by a deadly [[Nightshade thorn]], the young Ewoks [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]] and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] set out to find a cure. The only known antidote to Nightshade was sunnydew nectar, a substance in short supply. The reserve that Chirpa had stolen from Gantu in years prior had been exhausted, so the two young Ewoks had to find an alternate source. Learning of Chirpa's daring raid on Gantu's lair from Logray, Wicket stole the rainbow crystal from under Ebab's nose, and together with Logray, set out for Zandor.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

After walking across Zandor, they encountered the Zandor Rockers, who brought them to Gantu's cave. The Ogre was not to be found in his residence, so Wicket ran over to steal the nectar and save Kneesaa. However, Gantu soon returned from his errands with a club in his hand, ready to kill the Ewoks. Gantu protected the nectar, which he referred to as "light water", so that the Ewoks could not steal it. However, when Wicket distracted the Ogre by showing him the rainbow crystal, Gantu dropped the nectar and ran to reclaim the crystal. Wicket threw the crystal to Teebo, who caught it.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

[[File:MeGlow-ME1.png|thumb|left|220px|Gantu relishes his newfound glow]]
However, Gantu was determined not to lose the crystal again, and grabbed Teebo. The Ewok dropped the crystal, breaking it. After this, the Ogre prepared to eat the two young Ewoks. As Wicket and Teebo ran, a Zandor Rocker dealt Gantu a distracting wound on the foot. Gantu accidentally spilled the sunnydew nectar on himself, causing him to glow. The spilled nectar began to create new sunnydews. However, sunnydew flowers had a long life cycle before blooming, and the two Ewoks did not have the time to save Kneesaa. The Zandor Rockers created a tunnel to transport the Ewoks under the Gorge, and small enough that Gantu could not fit in it. The Ewoks thanked the Zandor Rockers and left. Later, a plant that Wicket had picked up was found to be a sunnydew, which saved Kneesaa. Gantu never crossed the Gorge again, alleviating the Ewoks' fear of the giant Ogre.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|We can't stand him!|A Zandor Rocker|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
During his first raid on Bright Tree Village, Gantu was feared by all Ewoks. The Ogre even consumed many Ewoks, which struck much fear in the sentients. He was remembered as a giant monster to all the residents of Bright Tree Village. In addition to this, Gantu stole the Ewoks' supplies of honey, putting many Ewoks in danger of starvation. To the Zandor Rockers, Gantu was also a monster, but for a different reason. The Ogre raided the Rockers' villages of all their sunnydew flowers and nectar. Gantu used this nectar to light his cave through the night.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Me keep! Me afraid of dark!|Gantu|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
Gantu had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and cream skin. He was considered to be evil by the residents of Bright Tree Village and the Zandor Rockers. The Ogre constantly raided their villages, stealing the precious honey of the Ewoks and the sunnydew nectar of the Zandor Rockers. He was hated by both civilizations. Gantu was also known to be afraid of the dark. Instead of using the nectar's healing powers to clean his wounds and cure himself of illnesses, he used its glowing powers to light up his cave. Gantu speech used "me" in place of "I", an example of the Ogre's ineptitude with pronouns.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

Another characteristic of Gantu was that he was short-tempered and had a large appetite, though he refused to do any work in procuring his own food. Instead of acquiring his own sustenance or asking the Ewoks for honey, Gantu raided their village to feed himself. A similar situation arose when Gantu did not bother to ask the Zandor Rockers for their sunnydew flowers, but instead stole them.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gantu was created by writer [[David Manak]] as the antagonist of [[Ewoks (1985) 1|''Ewoks'' 1]], the first issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']].<ref name="Ewoks 1" /> The book was penciled by [[Warren Kremer]] and published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[May 1]], [[1985]].<ref name="Comic date">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54232/star_wars_ewoks_1985_1|text=Star Wars: Ewoks (1985) #1}}</ref>
